Introduction to Lamb County

Arrows in Lamb County

Arrow #61: Littlefield

More Details for Arrow #61

Coordinates: 33.9109395, -102.3269202

Installed: July 2, 2013

Marker Text:

None

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #62: Olton

More Details for Arrow #62

Coordinates: 34.1819261, -102.1347788

Installed: July 2, 2013

Marker Text:

In 1877, Comanches hunting for bison skirmished with buffalo hunters and retreated to sand hills S. of Olton

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #63: Rocky Ford

More Details for Arrow #63

Coordinates: 34.0801098, -102.3248905

Installed: July 2, 2013

Marker Text:

None

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #64: Earth

More Details for Arrow #64

Coordinates: 34.2331752, -102.4101562

Installed: July 2, 2013

Marker Text:

Comanches followed Blackwater Draw to camp by spring-fed Spring Lake and hunt in the sand hills south of Earth

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #65: Sudan

More Details for Arrow #65

Coordinates: 34.0672687, -102.5243412

Installed: July 2, 2013

Marker Text:

Comanches used lush grassland in Sudan area as waypoint for trading horses & cattle with Comancheros

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
